x.com/thestrokes

"Going Shopping"

Starring Walton Goggins

Directed by Johann Rashid

Produced by Nick Ventura

LYRICS:
Like a tiger they will chase you down
With words instead of claws

They will seduce you till you reach the point
to let yourself get mauled 

The worse reality gets the less you wanna hear about it
Solidarity can be difficult when you’ve got cool stuff to lose

I wanna be a 7-foot zombie
the pay is low but I gotta do something
I’m at the mall, the song is bumping. There goes my future wife in a little red jumpsuit
I’m going away to the country
Don’t wander off too far
I’m going out my mind
Throwin' all my plans out the window
Don’t wanna waste my life
I’ll see you on the other side

I’ve been thinking about what I want to say
But I’m an old man now, at least that’s what they tell me anyway

We’ve been expanding on our greatness
Building future ruins
We’re building castles from the bones of dead trees
Molded from the shattered houses of the dead sea

I moved away to the country
I had to change my way
But I kinda miss you now

Stockbrokers flying out the window, I kinda miss that sound
'Don't wanna wake up Pa'

- 

I can’t wait, I’m going shopping
I’m at the mall, and the song is bumpin’
I want to be a 7-foot starfish
above the law like good little puppet

I’m going back to the city
I’m ‘bout to lose my mind
I’m gonna stay alive
I’m climbing out through the window
I miss the shops and malls
I’m gonna meet you there

Still throwing my phone out the window
I’m gonna soothe my soul
Can’t wait I’m going shopping

If you’re better than me you don’t have to judge me

snippet.title: "Scratches" - Marsh Family parody of Clarence Carter's "Patches" (about Trump's Reflecting Pool)
snippet.description:
"Patches" with its soaring vocals and soulful energy, and its sad and empathetic storyline about a farmboy from Alabama being given a mission by his dying father, has always been a favourite for us. Sung by the late Clarence Carter - who sadly died just last month, aged 90 - it was written by lead singer of "Chairmen of the Board" General Johnson along with Ron Dunbar in 1970, before Carter turned it in to a blues hit. 

We are using it for a much more mundane topic than the affliction of southern rural poverty on a family, which is to reflect on Trump's disastrous ($14m and counting) renovation attempts of the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool in Washington DC, which will now not be complete in time for the anniversary celebrations. The story has travelled around the world and prompted lots of hilarious online memes and commentaries, being such an accessible and colourful microcosm of his presidency and its impact. The song pretty much speaks for itself, but in case you need any background on the events:

- https://www.bbc.co.uk/news/articles/c5yzpjd1njpo
- https://www.theguardian.com/us-news/2026/jun/23/donald-trump-reflecting-pool-metaphor
- https://www.forbes.com/sites/maryroeloffs/2026/06/26/trump-says-more-reflecting-pool-arrests-coming-these-people-are-sick/

We ummed and aahhed about trying accents, replicating the voiceover parts of the track, and attempting some light dance moves. But it was too hot to faff around, so we just fudged whatever seemed to amuse us. Apologies to Americans, esp. in the South. 

___________________________ 

Order our new album "Hollow Chapters" (came out 27 March): 
DIGITAL: https://orcd.co/hollowchapters 
PHYSICAL: CDs or LPs: https://themarshfamily.bandcamp.com/album/hollow-chapters

___________________________ 

Support us here: 
- PayPal to leave us a tip jar: https://paypal.me/marshfamilysongs 
- Patreon for ongoing support & bonuses: https://www.patreon.com/marshfamilysongs 

Find our other channels here: https://linktr.ee/marshfamilysongs 

Follow us here: 
- https://www.marshfamilysongs.com 
- https://www.facebook.com/MarshFamilySongs/ 
- https://www.instagram.com/marshfamilysongs/ 
- https://twitter.com/MarshSongs 
- https://www.threads.net/@marshfamilysongs 
- https://www.tiktok.com/@marshfamilysongs 
- https://bsky.app/profile/marshfamilysongs.bsky.social 
- https://open.spotify.com/artist/6AYmbCwi92V29y69lkRkRa 

___________________________ 

LYRICS

There’s a pond in the states with a lot of water
That’s alarming way more folk than it should
It’s unattractive, provoking some ugly clashes
Donald wasn’t pleased about it
‘Cause deep down inside he was hurt
‘Cause he’d done all he could

Why, Donald had a great old plan!
He was reelin’ from his blunder with Iran, see
Renovation would make him glad
No-bid funder for some friends he had
A lot of money for the job, he raised
Sturdy paint was underlaid

Oh, pipes were sticking up from the ground
When they filled the pool up
‘Twas the talk of the town
One day, Donald thought the pool would knock ‘em dead 
But his plans unfolded
And with tears he said, he said

Scratches
Needed mendin’ on his pond
The pool had been abused
By vandals, oh, it wrecked the news

Some days later paint was peelin’ ‘way, and
It became a swamp you’d say
Oh, Trump told MAGA it was a terrorist coup, but
He said that without the slightest proof
Now every visitor can ridicule
His metaphorical reflecting pool

The guy who said that he’s drainin’ the swamp
You wouldn’t believe, it’s now awash with scum
Oh my, did you ever see a duck so dead?
We see how he lies on his concrete bed

He said: Patches 
Kept extending on his pond
He tried to make arrests
He mobilised the NPS

Every day they found the surface green!
So they tried to kinda bleach it clean, you see
There was a foulness all around DC
But this specific one was down to algae

Every night you heard ol’ Donald pray
Lord, give me strength to find someone to blame

But weeks have passed and he can bitch and moan
History gonna show it as a big self-own
The pool’s fecal, but the moral’s clear:
No one cleans up like a racketeer!

Leavin’ patches 
Of contamination
Trump nevers turn things blue!
I wonder who expects him to?!

Batshit!  
Just a neverendin’ scum
A noxious sludge egressed
Who failed his own Narcissus test!



___________________________ 

THANKS SO MUCH TO PATREONS (HOWLERS below):

Jeremy Sedgley,  
John Hanger,  
Stephen Shuttleworth,  
Sacramento Scott,  
Christian Schranz,  
Peter Green,  
Richard Boutbee,  
LisaMarie Fitzpatrick,  
Ed Rocheck,  
Wolfgang Tampier,  
Dan McColm,  
Douglas Lind,  
David Ryan,  
Simon Haslam,  
Lew,  
Mike Smith,  
Andy Riches,  
Cliff Hope,  
Carol Leff,  
Geeta & Karen (Alyssa Sangeeta Jacobson Memorial), 
David Patrao, 
Grandad

#LetsGameItOut

I Made This Guy Give Birth to Over 150 Kids in Paralives - Let's Game It Out

----------

Check it out on Steam ► https://store.steampowered.com/app/1118520/Paralives/

NOTE: This was the Steam description as of June 28, 2026. Since the game is in early access, some of these things may have changed!

More about Paralives (from Steam):

Hop on, we’re leaving, and your new life awaits! Paralives is a sandbox life simulation game with a great cast of Townies to meet, as well as a vibrant open-world town to explore and make your own. You will feel right at home, should you choose to embark.

What kind of life will you build?
Whether you focus on your career, finding love, building a family, or becoming the richest person in town, the story is yours to write in Paralives. But twists and forks in the road await. What will you leave behind?

You live and then you die. At least, do it in a nice house!
Thanks to an ever-evolving catalog of furniture, decor and clutter objects, staging the perfect homes for your Parafolks will be a breeze: resize, recolor, stack and place items freely for a tactile building experience.

Paralives allows truly grid-less constructions with curved walls, split-level floors and more. Recreating your house or building an intricate 8-story mansion has never been this easy.

There are lives to create and stories to tell
You will be handed an advanced character creator called the Paramaker. Adjust the height of your Parafolks, tweak a wide array of facial and body features, design stylish outfits and create complex personalities. From head to toe, no small detail will be left out!

Key features
🏡 Explore an open-world town filled with shops, parks, restaurants and museums
👕 Create Parafolks of all looks and styles
💼 Find a job and try out flexible career progression paths
🧠 Discover unique personality traits, skills, wants, needs and emotions
❤️ Find love and make friends, or enemies
👨‍👩‍👦 Multiselect Parafolks and do anything as a group
👵 Grow up, have children and live a long life
🔨 Enjoy the customization and flexibility of our house-building tools
⚙️ Experiment with our in-game modding interface to edit or add content to the game
💌 Find community-made mods, houses and Parafolks through the Steam Workshop


More features and content will be added during Early Access and after through exclusively free updates, as we have pledged to our community.

To be added during the Early Access phase
Weather and seasons
Dogs, cats and horses
Cars and bikes
Boats and houseboats
Pools and swimming
Calendar and organizing social events (parties, weddings)
Story progression for NPCs
Family tree
Gardening and fishing
Tools to edit and create towns
More personality traits, wants, emotions, jobs, etc

snippet.title: The Last Samurai 2 (2026) | Tom Cruise, Arnold Schwarzenegger, Bryan Cranston | Concept Trailer
snippet.description:
#TheLastSamurai2 #tomcruise #concepttrailer 

Watch This The Last Samurai 2 Concept Trailer

What if Nathan Algren never truly left Japan and twenty-two years after the fall of the samurai, the warrior code he swore to honor called him back to fight one last time? This fan-made concept trailer imagines The Last Samurai 2 for 2026 where Tom Cruise returns as Algren, living in exile deep in the mountains of Japan, haunting a quiet village and carrying the memory of Katsumoto until a ruthless imperial general named Takeda Hiroshi played by Bryan Cranston launches a campaign to erase every remaining samurai bloodline and seize ancestral lands for industrial expansion. I personally created every storyboard and visual in this "What If" concept from scratch, building a sequel where Arnold Schwarzenegger plays Colonel Elias Crane, an American arms dealer who supplies Takeda with devastating Gatling guns and modern weapons, turning a cultural purge into an industrial slaughter that shatters Algren's seclusion when a neighboring village is burned to the ground and its people massacred before his eyes.

Emily Blunt plays Akemi, Katsumoto's fierce granddaughter and a self-taught swordswoman who carries her grandfather's blade but distrusts the foreigner who claims to have loved his ways, and her reluctant alliance with Algren becomes the emotional core of a story about legacy, grief, and the cost of keeping a dying code alive. Every visual in this HD concept trailer was designed and built by me to honor the breathtaking cinematography and emotional weight of the original Last Samurai while pushing the saga into a desperate final stand at an ancient mountain fortress where scattered ronin trained by Algren and Akemi face Takeda's advancing army and Crane's Gatling guns in a battle that tests the resistance to its breaking point. This Last Samurai 2 concept trailer ends with Algren defeating Takeda in single combat on a rain-soaked mountainside, Akemi holding her grandfather's blade upright at the gate wounded but unbroken, and the ronin who came back to fight one last time walking alone into the mountains once more, disappearing into the landscape that made him because some warriors are not meant to be remembered but to simply endure.

Disclaimer: This is entirely a fan-made concept trailer created for entertainment and creative purposes only. All storyboard work and visuals were originally created by me. This video is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or connected to Warner Bros., or any official The Last Samurai production or its rights holders.
